TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL 8 CIRCLES IS CALCULATED BY SUMMING THE AREAS OF EACH CIRCLE. THE RADIUS OF EACH CIRCLE INCREASES BY 10% FROM THE PREVIOUS ONE. THE FORMULA FOR THE AREA OF A CIRCLE IS πR². THE RADIUS OF THE 1ST CIRCLE IS r, THE 2ND IS 1.1r, THE 3RD IS (1.1)²r, AND SO ON UNTIL THE 8TH CIRCLE. THE TOTAL AREA IS πr²(1 + (1.1)² + (1.1)⁴ + ... + (1.1)¹⁴). THIS IS A GEOMETRIC SERIES WITH 8 TERMS. THE SUM OF THE SERIES IS [1 - (1.1)¹⁶]/[1 - 1.1]. THE TOTAL AREA IS πr² × [1 - (1.1)¹⁶]/[1 - 1.1]. THE FINAL EXPRESSION IS πr² × [1 - (1.1)¹⁶]/[-0.1].

THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE IS 2πR, WHERE R IS THE RADIUS OF THE 5TH CIRCLE. THE RADIUS OF THE 5TH CIRCLE IS (1.1)⁴r. THE CIRCUMFERENCE IS 2π(1.1)⁴r. THE TEXT CHARACTERS ARE SPACED WITH A 0.5 CM GAP. THE TOTAL LENGTH OCCUPIED BY EACH CHARACTER AND GAP IS 0.5 CM. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS THE CIRCUMFERENCE DIVIDED BY 0.5. THE CIRCUMFERENCE IS 2π(1.1)⁴r. DIVIDING BY 0.5 GIVES 4π(1.1)⁴r. THIS MUST BE AN INTEGER. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS THE FLOOR OF 4π(1.1)⁴r. IF r IS A VALUE THAT MAKES 4π(1.1)⁴r AN INTEGER, THEN THAT IS THE MAXIMUM NUMBER. OTHERWISE, IT IS THE INTEGER PART OF THE VALUE. THE ANSWER DEPENDS ON THE VALUE OF r. IF r IS CHOSEN TO MAKE 4π(1.1)
